correctness.justification: All 20 acceptance tests pass. Handles Unicode transliteration via NFKD, separator customization, max_length truncation with word boundary, empty inputs, and special character stripping correctly.

edge_cases.justification: All edge cases pass: empty string, whitespace-only, special-chars-only, max_length=0, single long word with no separator (hard truncate), exact fit, trailing separator after truncation, and word boundary truncation all handled correctly.

code_quality.justification: Clean structure, full type hints, idiomatic Python with re and unicodedata. Minor issue: `escaped_sep = re.escape(separator)` is computed but never used — dead code. Otherwise excellent naming and readability.

documentation.justification: Comprehensive module-level docstring and function docstring with Args, Returns sections. Inline comments explain each processing step clearly. Minor gap: no explicit note about the hard-truncate fallback behavior in the docstring.

test_coverage.justification: 17 tests covering happy path, Unicode transliteration, consecutive separators, empty/whitespace/special-char inputs, max_length edge cases (zero, exact fit, word boundary, hard truncate, trailing separator), custom separator, numbers, and mixed content. Very thorough coverage.

robustness.justification: Handles None-like empty string, max_length=0, and all-non-ASCII inputs gracefully. Returns empty string appropriately. Minor gap: no validation for negative max_length or non-string inputs, and no error raised for invalid separator types.
